title: Repository Guidelines
text: R/ organized durable workflow domains. Current modules include preprocessing (preprocessing.R), clustering/integration (analysis_clustering.R), differential expression (analysis_de.R), enrichment (analysis_enrichment.R), metrics/composition (analysis_metrics.R), example data (data_examples.R), IO (data_io.R), interpretation (interpretation.R), package helpers (package_tools.R), signatures (signatures.R), visualization (visualization.R), small utilities (utils.R). man/ contains roxygen2-generated .Rd files. Treat generated output keep synchronized roxygen comments R/. tests/testthat/ currently small unit-test surface; add focused tests near behavior change. vignettes/ contains longer workflows. Keep chunks check-safe avoid unconditional network access heavyweight setup examples. data/ stores package datasets. docs/codex/ stores package-maintainer Codex docs modernization memory; already excluded package builds via .Rbuildignore. inst/codex/project-template/ stores shipped initialized-project governance template. inst/codex/package-skills/ stores shipped package-usage Codex skills. Keep repository-planning modernization memory installed user assets. _pkgdown.yml .github/workflows/ define package website CI entry points.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs local test suite. Rscript -e 'testthat::test_local(filter = "composition", stop_on_failure = TRUE)' runs current focused tests iterating composition-related changes. Rscript -e '(requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) devtools::document() else stop("devtools installed")' regenerates NAMESPACE man/ roxygen changes. R CMD build . builds package tarball. R CMD check ---manual Shennong_*.tar.gz preferred package check successful build.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Preserve public behavior unless change explicitly justified, documented, validated. Prefer small, reviewable refactors repo-wide rewrites. Keep exported user-facing functions strict sn_verb_noun naming family. introduce camelCase, dot.case, mixed prefixes. Reuse existing sn_* naming families whenever possible. Keep internal helpers unexported clearly named. Prefer explicit namespace usage roxygen @importFrom entries hidden dependencies. Keep examples deterministic safe package checks; wrap network- dependency-heavy examples \dontrun{} make conditional. Avoid introducing new package dependencies unless remove concrete maintenance problem solved simply.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Add update tests changing behavior risky areas. Prefer lightweight tests require external downloads optional heavyweight packages unless function contract truly depends . Run narrowest relevant tests first, rerun full local suite closing milestone. roxygen, exports, package metadata change, regenerate documentation rerun relevant validation commands. add change user-facing function, parameter, stored-result schema, workflow, must also update relevant pkgdown article(s) shipped Codex assets inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/ change set affected. user-facing change set must also update NEWS.md release notes reflect shipped behavior. New stored-result retrieval interpretation features must documented user level least one concrete example showing discover stored result retrieve Seurat object. updating user-facing feature, rebuild pkgdown locally rendered site matches current package sources. Updating vignette/reference source files sufficient.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Keep modernization step self-contained: one small change set, one validation pass, one update docs/codex/Status.md docs/codex/Decisions.md. overwrite unrelated working tree changes; repository may dirty. Record compatibility notes, validation commands, rationale non-obvious changes docs/codex/. Breaking changes require explicit documentation docs/codex/Decisions.md NEWS.md. Use Conventional Commits commit messages. Preferred commit format: <type>(<scope>): <summary>. Typical types repo: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, build, ci, chore. Keep summary imperative specific, example: feat(clustering): consolidate quick integration workflows. change breaking, add ! type scope explain break commit body NEWS.md.

title: Repository Guidelines
text: Likely public API surface: IO (sn_read, sn_write, dataset loading), Seurat initialization/normalization/QC, clustering/integration/annotation, plotting, signatures/enrichment, composition/metrics. Current high-risk zones data import/export, heavyweight optional-package integrations, schema stored object@misc. Current modernization order : inventory API, stabilize tests, normalize metadata namespace handling, reorganize source files without changing behavior, tighten internals documentation. Repository-internal developer memory stays docs/codex/ AGENTS.md. Installed-package user assets belong inst/codex/project-template/ inst/codex/package-skills/.

code: library(Shennong)  pbmc <- sn_load_data("pbmc1k") pbmc <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(pbmc, project = "pbmc1k", species = "human") pbmc <- sn_filter_cells(   pbmc,   features = c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"),   plot = FALSE ) pbmc <- sn_filter_genes(pbmc, min_cells = 3, plot = FALSE) pbmc <- sn_run_cluster(   pbmc,   normalization_method = "seurat",   resolution = 0.6 )  sn_plot_dim(pbmc, group_by = "seurat_clusters", label = TRUE)

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: sn_remove_ambient_contamination() exposes unified interface multiple methods. decontX, switch method: write decontX output back Seurat object, corrected counts stored separate layer default corrected per-cell totals added metadata.
code: filtered_path <- sn_load_data(   dataset = "pbmc3k",   matrix_type = "filtered",   return_object = FALSE )  raw_path <- sn_load_data(   dataset = "pbmc3k",   matrix_type = "raw",   return_object = FALSE )  ambient_counts <- sn_remove_ambient_contamination(   x = filtered_path,   raw = raw_path,   method = "soupx",   return_object = FALSE ) corrected_counts <- sn_remove_ambient_contamination(   x = ambient_counts,   method = "decontx",   return_object = FALSE )

title: Toolkit for Interpreting Omics Data
text: sn_run_cluster() main clustering entry point single datasets batch-aware workflows.
code: pbmc1k <- sn_load_data("pbmc1k") pbmc3k <- sn_load_data("pbmc3k")  obj1 <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(pbmc1k, project = "pbmc1k") obj1$sample <- "pbmc1k" obj2 <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(pbmc3k, project = "pbmc3k") obj2$sample <- "pbmc3k"  merged <- merge(obj1, y = obj2, add.cell.ids = c("pbmc1k", "pbmc3k"))  merged <- sn_run_cluster(   object = merged,   batch = "sample",   normalization_method = "seurat",   resolution = 0.6 )

title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de

NULL, function infers analysis arguments. ident_1, ident_2 Group labels direct contrasts. required "contrast" "pseudobulk" analyses. group_by Optional metadata column defines groups compare. omitted, Seurat identities used. subset_by Optional metadata column used repeat contrast within subset, example per cell type. subset_levels Optional character vector subset values analyze. Defaults observed values subset_by. sample_col Metadata column containing sample IDs. Required "pseudobulk" analyses. assay Assay used DE analysis. Defaults "RNA". layer Assay layer used DE analysis. Defaults "data" marker contrast analyses "counts" pseudobulk analyses. features Optional feature subset test. method Statistical method. "markers" "contrast", can Seurat test.use value "COSGR" marker discovery. "pseudobulk", choose one "DESeq2", "edgeR", "limma". only_pos Whether return positive markers. Defaults TRUE "markers" FALSE otherwise. logfc_threshold, min_pct Standard Seurat marker filtering arguments. p_val_cutoff Adjusted p-value threshold used storing result metadata. de_logfc Absolute log fold-change threshold used storing result metadata. min_cells_per_sample Minimum cells required sample/group pseudobulk profile retained. store_name Key used object@misc$de_results. return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object stored DE results. title: Run differential expression analysis on a Seurat object — sn_find_de
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <- counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <- counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20    obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)    marker_tbl <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     return_object = FALSE,     verbose = FALSE   )   head(marker_tbl)    obj <- sn_find_de(     obj,     analysis = "markers",     group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data",     min_pct = 0,     logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers",     return_object = TRUE,     verbose = FALSE   )   names(obj@misc$de_results) } #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:39] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:39] Running QC metrics for human ... #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:39] Seurat object initialization complete. #> For a (much!) faster implementation of the Wilcoxon Rank Sum Test, #> (default method for FindMarkers) please install the presto package #> -------------------------------------------- #> install.packages('devtools') #> devtools::install_github('immunogenomics/presto') #> -------------------------------------------- #> After installation of presto, Seurat will automatically use the more  #> efficient implementation (no further action necessary). #> This message will be shown once per session #> [1] "celltype_markers"

what: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: function downloads (already cached) loads processed single-cell RNA-seq example datasets Zenodo-backed registry. current registry contains PBMC example datasets (pbmc1k, pbmc3k, pbmc4k, pbmc8k), interface intentionally generalized additional example datasets can added later without introducing another top-level loader function.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
code: sn_load_data(   dataset = "pbmc3k",   matrix_type = c("filtered", "raw"),   save_dir = "~/.shennong/data",   return_object = TRUE,   species = NULL )

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: dataset Character scalar. example dataset load. Currently one "pbmc1k", "pbmc3k", "pbmc4k", "pbmc8k". Default: "pbmc3k". matrix_type Character scalar. matrix type load. One : "filtered": filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (Cell Ranger filtered barcodes). "raw": raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 (unfiltered barcodes; useful ambient RNA correction tools SoupX). Default: "filtered". save_dir Character scalar. Local cache directory downloaded Zenodo files stored. directory created exist. Default: "~/.shennong/data". return_object Logical. TRUE (default), function returns -memory object. FALSE, function ensures file downloaded locally returns (invisibly) local file path. species Character scalar. Species label passed sn_initialize_seurat_object() constructing Seurat objects (.e. matrix_type == "filtered"). Ignored matrix_type == "raw". NULL, use dataset default example-data registry.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: One : matrix_type == "filtered" return_object = TRUE: Seurat object. matrix_type == "raw" return_object = TRUE: sparse count matrix (e.g. dgCMatrix). return_object = FALSE: local file path cached .h5 file, returned invisibly.

title: Load example datasets from Zenodo — sn_load_data
text: Instead storing serialized R objects, function caches original Zenodo .h5 files locally reproducibility version consistency. requested, dynamically constructs returns either: Seurat object (filtered data), sparse count matrix (raw data). Users can also choose download/cache data without loading memory. datasets re-aligned using Cell Ranger v9.0.1 custom reference based GENCODE v48 (GRCh38.p14). Caching strategy function caches original files Zenodo first use: example, loading pbmc3k may see: return_object = TRUE, cached .h5 file read fly: matrix_type == "filtered": Seurat object constructed via sn_initialize_seurat_object(). matrix_type == "raw": function returns sparse count matrix (typically dgCMatrix), suitable ambient RNA estimation / SoupX workflows. return_object = FALSE, object constructed; file ensured exist locally.
code: {dataset}_{matrix_type}_feature_bc_matrix.h5 ~/.shennong/data/ |- pbmc1k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc1k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc3k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc3k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc4k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc4k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5 |- pbmc8k_filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5 \- pbmc8k_raw_feature_bc_matrix.h5

code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(20 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 20, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c(     paste0("GENE", 1:14),     "CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"   )   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] <- counts[c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC"), 1:12] + 20   counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] <- counts[c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA"), 13:24] + 20   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   evidence <- sn_prepare_annotation_evidence(obj, de_name = "celltype_markers", cluster_col = "cell_type")   names(evidence) } #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:51] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:51] Running QC metrics for human ... #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:51] Seurat object initialization complete. #> [1] "task"             "cluster_col"      "source_de_name"   "analysis_method"  #> [5] "species"          "cluster_summary"  "top_marker_table" "caveats"

text: function main clustering entry point Shennong. batch = NULL, performs single-dataset clustering either standard Seurat workflow SCTransform workflow. batch supplied, performs Harmony-based integration followed clustering UMAP.

title: Run clustering for a single dataset or Harmony integration workflow — sn_run_cluster
code: sn_run_cluster(   object,   batch = NULL,   normalization_method = c("seurat", "scran", "sctransform"),   nfeatures = 3000,   vars_to_regress = NULL,   resolution = 0.8,   hvg_group_by = NULL,   block_genes = c("heatshock", "ribo", "mito", "tcr", "immunoglobulins", "pseudogenes"),   theta = 2,   group_by_vars = NULL,   npcs = 50,   dims = NULL,   species = NULL,   assay = "RNA",   layer = "counts",   return_cluster = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run clustering for a single dataset or Harmony integration workflow — sn_run_cluster
text: object Seurat object. batch column name object@meta.data specifying batch info. NULL, integration performed. normalization_method One "seurat", "scran", "sctransform". SCTransform scran workflows currently supported batch = NULL. nfeatures Number variable features select. vars_to_regress Covariates regress ScaleData. resolution Resolution parameter FindClusters. hvg_group_by Optional metadata column used compute highly variable genes within groups merging ranking . Use NULL compute HVGs full object. block_genes Either character vector predefined bundled signature categories (example c("ribo","mito")) custom vector gene symbols exclude HVGs. theta theta parameter harmony::RunHarmony, controlling batch diversity preservation vs. correction. group_by_vars Optional column name character vector passed harmony::RunHarmony(group..vars = ...). Defaults batch. npcs Number PCs compute RunPCA. dims numeric vector PCs (dimensions) use neighbor search, clustering, UMAP. species Optional species label. Used block genes must resolved built-signatures. assay Assay used clustering. Defaults "RNA". layer Layer used input count matrix. Defaults "counts". return_cluster TRUE, return cluster assignments. verbose Whether print/log progress messages.

title: Run clustering for a single dataset or Harmony integration workflow — sn_run_cluster
text: Seurat object clustering results embeddings, cluster vector return_cluster = TRUE.

code: sn_store_enrichment(   object,   result,   store_name = "default",   analysis = c("ora", "gsea"),   database = "GOBP",   species = NULL,   source_de_name = NULL,   gene_col = "gene",   score_col = NULL,   return_object = TRUE )

title: Store an enrichment result on a Seurat object — sn_store_enrichment
text: object Seurat object. result enrichment result object data frame coercible .data.frame(). store_name Name used object@misc$enrichment_results. analysis One "ora" "gsea". database Database used enrichment, example "GOBP". species Species label used enrichment run. source_de_name Optional stored DE result name produced input ranked gene list gene set. gene_col Column containing gene symbols enrichment input came data frame. score_col Column containing ranking scores GSEA inputs. return_object TRUE, return updated Seurat object.

title: Store an enrichment result on a Seurat object — sn_store_enrichment
text: Seurat object stored-result list.

title: Store an enrichment result on a Seurat object — sn_store_enrichment
code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(10 * 12, lambda = 1), nrow = 10, ncol = 12)   rownames(counts) <- c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "LTB", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA", "LYZ", "ACTB", "MALAT1")   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:12)   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   enrich_tbl <- tibble::tibble(     ID = c("GO:0001", "GO:0002"),     Description = c("immune response", "lymphocyte activation"),     NES = c(2.1, 1.7),     p.adjust = c(0.01, 0.03)   )   obj <- sn_store_enrichment(obj, enrich_tbl, store_name = "demo_gsea")   names(obj@misc$enrichment_results) } #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:57] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:57] Running QC metrics for human ... #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:57] Seurat object initialization complete. #> [1] "demo_gsea"

code: if (requireNamespace("Seurat", quietly = TRUE)) {   counts <- matrix(rpois(10 * 24, lambda = 1), nrow = 10, ncol = 24)   rownames(counts) <- c("CD3D", "CD3E", "TRAC", "LTB", "MS4A1", "CD79A", "HLA-DRA", "LYZ", "ACTB", "MALAT1")   colnames(counts) <- paste0("cell", 1:24)   obj <- sn_initialize_seurat_object(counts, species = "human")   obj$cell_type <- rep(c("Tcell", "Bcell"), each = 12)   Seurat::Idents(obj) <- obj$cell_type   obj <- Seurat::NormalizeData(obj, verbose = FALSE)   obj <- sn_find_de(obj, analysis = "markers", group_by = "cell_type",     layer = "data", min_pct = 0, logfc_threshold = 0,     store_name = "celltype_markers", return_object = TRUE, verbose = FALSE   )   prompt <- sn_write_figure_legend(obj, cluster_de_name = "celltype_markers", cluster_col = "cell_type", return_prompt = TRUE)   prompt$task } #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:58] Initializing Seurat object for project: Shennong #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:58] Running QC metrics for human ... #> INFO [2026-03-24 21:23:58] Seurat object initialization complete. #> Warning: No DE genes identified #> [1] "figure_legend"

title: Version 0.1.1
text: Released 2026-03-19.

title: Version 0.1.1
text: Automatic human/mouse species inference sn_get_species() using hom_genes mitochondrial naming patterns. layer-aware pkgdown article covering inferred species, non-default count layers, stored differential-expression metadata. Additional regression tests species inference, DE metadata, BPCells-backed Seurat layers.

title: Version 0.1.1
text: sn_initialize_seurat_object() now attempts species inference deciding whether compute species-specific QC metrics. sn_get_signatures() now uses package-local fallback signatures core categories needed Shennong workflows SignatuR unavailable. Stored DE results now include schema provenance metadata package version, timestamp, assay/layer context, threshold settings. sn_plot_*() helpers now treat catplot optional enhancement rather mandatory dependency.

title: Version 0.1.1
text: Fixed sn_remove_ambient_contamination() BPCells-backed Seurat layers materializing BPCells matrices passing decontX. Fixed optional dependency handling missing SignatuR longer breaks core initialization clustering paths. Fixed SoupX validation order missing raw input reported package availability issues.

title: Version 0.1.0
text: sn_load_data() primary example-data loader. Consolidated clustering sn_run_cluster(). unified ambient contamination interface SoupX decontX. Missing help pages exported functions, CI scaffolding, richer README, updated package metadata current R syntax requirements.
